method_atom: A linear (kinematic) eigenvalue analysis of a self-exciting feedback mechanism confined to a bounded domain, in which an anisotropic feedback tensor derived from an underlying structured flow drives exponential amplification of a divergence-free vector field. One studies how variations in the domain's geometry (aspect ratio, thickness of an annular region) and the spatial dependence of the feedback coefficient select the dominant growing spatial mode, its symmetry orientation, and whether the amplified solution is stationary or oscillatory, with a critical geometric control parameter marking a transition between competing mode types.
object_atom: The magnetic field induced by kinematic alpha-squared dynamos with an anisotropic alpha effect in a finite cylinder or annular space, motivated by laboratory dynamo experiments (the Karlsruhe facility) and the search for Earth-like planetary dynamo behaviour.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the bounded-domain linear self-excitation eigenvalue problem and the way domain geometry selects the dominant mode's symmetry and steady-vs-oscillatory character; the domain-specific part is the magnetic dynamo / anisotropic-alpha / Karlsruhe-facility system.
